title: philydrella pygmaea subsp. minima
intro: Philydrella pygmaea subsp. minima, also known as the Dwarf Greenhood orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Philydrella pygmaea subsp. minima include its potential to aid in the treatment of various health issues, such as colds, fever, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a dried extract or infusion, often combined with other herbs to create remedies for these conditions.
